metric_name: PC lifecycle cost per user per year
notes: Aberdeen cited industry data on PC lifecycle costs driving NC adoption thesis.

metric_value: 4th highest volume worldwide
notes: StrongARM ranked 4th behind SGI/MIPS (Nintendo), PowerPC, and Intel x86.

metric_value: Under $50
notes: StrongARM priced under $50 in quantity, enabling sub-$750 NC desktops.

metric_value: ~6 watts
notes: Digital NC consumed ~6 watts total, enabling battery operation in mobile versions.

metric_name: CaffeineMark Java benchmark: StrongARM vs SGI/Sun workstations
metric_value: >2x throughput
notes: StrongARM 233 MHz delivered more than twice the CaffeineMark throughput of SGI and Sun Ultra workstations and Intel 200 MHz Pentium Pro.

metric_value: Most comprehensive NC software suite in industry
notes: Aberdeen awarded NCI software suite highest marks for architecture and breadth of functionality for a first release.

metric_value: Wolf in sheep's clothing
notes: Aberdeen concluded NetPC retained all Microsoft OS maintenance issues and failed to address true ownership cost drivers.

metric_name: NC Access ROM OS memory footprint
metric_value: Less than 500K
notes: NCI's NC Access ROM-based OS required less than 500K of memory.

metric_value: Public-private key auth, digital signatures, bank balances
notes: NC Card stored authentication data, digital signatures, and bank balances in credit-card form factor.

metric_value: 10 (Ten Commandments)
notes: Aberdeen identified 10 design goals for the Digital/NCI NC environment: Affordable, Economical, Simple, Useable, Powerful, Scaleable, Secure, Open, Flexible, Deliverable.

metric_name: Annual electricity savings per NC vs PC
notes: NC was projected to save $125 per user per year in electricity versus typical PC.

metric_value: NC manufactured by Funai and Aranex; distributed via terminal channels
notes: DEC outsourced NC manufacturing to Funai and Aranex; bundled NC+server+services packages.

metric_value: Software integrator for OEM hardware partners
notes: NCI positioned as software integrator, not hardware manufacturer; OEMs such as DEC built hardware.

metric_value: Financial services, broad service industry, manufacturing floor, field service
notes: Aberdeen predicted DEC/NCI success in financial services and service industry; mobile NCs in manufacturing.

metric_value: Strong synergy; better than either alone
notes: Aberdeen concluded DEC/NCI partnership was engineered for real collaboration, not a 'marketing marriage of convenience.'

metric_name: Digital/NCI as premier NC solution by end 1997
metric_value: Likely to be viewed as one of the premier NC solutions by end of 1997
notes: Aberdeen predicted Digital/NCI products would be viewed as premier NC solution by end of 1997.

metric_name: NC as practical desktop choice for enterprises
metric_value: NCs are practical choice; no-brainer for terminal replacements
notes: Aberdeen recommended IT executives consider NCs for desktop computing and strongly recommended for terminal replacements.

metric_value: NetPC will have little change on PC direction over 18 months
notes: Aberdeen predicted NetPC announcement would not significantly alter NC vs PC dynamics.

metric_name: Java performance headroom of StrongARM NC
metric_value: Sufficient headroom for 5-year depreciation including video/Internet telephony
notes: Aberdeen endorsed StrongARM performance as providing 5-year headroom for emerging Java-intensive applications.

metric_name: DEC as NC industry hardware leader
metric_value: Digital has opportunity to become one of NC industry's overall hardware leaders in 1997
notes: Aberdeen predicted DEC could challenge IBM and Sun as NC hardware leader in 1997.

metric_value: Oracle shut down NCI in 1999 after ~$175M in losses
notes: Oracle renamed NCI assets for interactive TV in 1999 after spending ~$175M. NC concept failed in 1997-1999 timeframe.

metric_value: Compaq acquired DEC for $9.6B in June 1998
notes: Largest tech acquisition at the time. DEC's NC and Alpha businesses absorbed; Alpha later killed.

metric_value: DEC sold StrongARM and semiconductor division to Intel in 1997 patent settlement
notes: Intel acquired DEC's Hudson fab and StrongARM for $700M as part of patent lawsuit settlement in Oct 1997. Intel shipped SA-1100 in 1998; XScale succeeded StrongARM in 2000.

metric_name: NC concept vindicated via cloud computing
metric_value: Cloud computing, Chromebooks, VDI vindicated NC architectural thesis
notes: Google Chromebook (2011), cloud computing, and VDI/DaaS realized the NC vision ~15 years later. Eric Schmidt (ex-Sun) led Google to realize NC concept.

metric_value: Oracle acquired Sun in 2010 for $7.4B
notes: Sun, the other major NC proponent (JavaStation), was acquired by Oracle — the same company that tried NCI.

metric_value: Intel replaced StrongARM with XScale architecture in 2000
notes: Intel replaced StrongARM with XScale (StrongARM-derived) in 2000. XScale dominated Pocket PCs/PDAs before ARM Cortex architecture superseded it.

metric_value: Dumb terminals and Intel 286/386/486 PCs unable to run Windows 95
notes: Aberdeen identified specific upgrade targets: legacy terminals and pre-Pentium PCs as prime NC replacement candidates.
